notes: Fixed the close-and-redeem double-counting. Previously 'close & redeem all' both (a) created a redemption transaction that mutated the investment's STARTING balance — corrupting past months like May — and (b) fired the close event's redemption into savings, so the money was removed twice and past months shifted. Now the close lifecycle event is the SINGLE money-mover: it redeems the full projected balance to savings and zeroes the instrument from the close month onward, leaving past months untouched. The accompanying Journal redemption entry is created directly and tagged source:'instClose' so it is fully visible in the Journal and the instrument's history, but every balance/cash calculation (applyTransactionDelta starting-balance mutation, engine savings overlay, savings reverse-walk, income-inflow fold) skips it to avoid the double-count. The investment running-balance reconstruction now short-circuits closed investments: months before the close show the engine's natural value, the close month onward shows 0. Net result: close RD in June → May stays 1,48,000, June onward 0, savings receives the balance once, and a redemption entry appears in the Journal.
